sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 48, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal also +3 cols right (VERIFIED steps 1-4)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal also -3 cols left (VERIFIED steps 5-6)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal also +3 rows down (VERIFIED steps 7-10)', 'UP': 'Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal also -3 rows up (HYPOTHESIS)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black 6x6 shape with 5 white dot features, moves with arrow keys', 'charcoal_shape': 'Charcoal object that moves same direction as avatar by 3 pixels, initially at offset', 'yellow_shape': 'Target object at (45,51), 45 pixels, L-shaped, 6x6', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at cols 30-32, changes shape as objects cross it'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63, border', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63, border'}
Current level:
- World model: Avatar overshot target and merged with bottom black strip (node 7, 103 pixels, rows 54-63, cols 0-62). Target yellow at (45,51). Avatar likely stuck at bottom edge.
- Goal model: Move avatar to overlap with yellow target at (45,51)
- Recent findings: ['Avatar moved too far down and merged with bottom border', 'Need to go UP to reach target row 45']
- Open questions: ['What completes the level - overlap with target?', 'Can avatar escape bottom border merge?', 'Does SPACE or MOUSE do something special?']
- Plan: Try UP moves to get avatar back to target row. If UP doesn't work, try SPACE or MOUSE.
- Strategy log: ['Overshot target by going DOWN 7 + RIGHT 6 from (33,42) to (54,60), then corrected with UP 3 + LEFT 3']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 47 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 1.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image after LEFT, I can see:
- The yellow L-shape is now to the RIGHT of the avatar
- The avatar moved left, and the target moved to the right side
Let me track the target's position relative to the avatar:
1. Initially: target was to the right of avatar
2. After DOWN: target moved above avatar
3. After LEFT: target moved to the right of avatar
It seems like the target is always positioned at a fixed offset from the avatar, rotating based on the last movement direction. Let me think about this more carefully.
Actually, looking at the image, the target seems to be positioned:
- After DOWN: target was above avatar
- After LEFT: target is to the right of avatar
This looks like the target is always 90 degrees clockwise from the direction of the last move:
- DOWN → target above (opposite direction)
- LEFT → target to the right (90 degrees clockwise from left)
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the target is always positioned in the direction opposite to the last move:
- After DOWN: target above (opposite of down = up) ✓
- After LEFT: target to the right (opposite of left = right) ✓
So the target is always opposite to the last move direction! This means:
- If I move UP, the target will be below
- If I move RIGHT, the target will be to the left
Now, what's the goal? Maybe I need to get the target to overlap with the charcoal shape? Or maybe I need to get the target to a specific position?
Let me look at the charcoal shape - it's an L-shape at the bottom left. The target is also an L-shape. Maybe the goal is to match the target's position/orientation to the charcoal shape?
Or maybe the goal is simpler - maybe I need to press SPACE when the target is in a specific position relative to the avatar, like when it's directly overlapping or adjacent.
Let me try a different approach. Let me see what happens if I move the avatar to the left side, near the charcoal shape, and then press SPACE.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the image more carefully. The target (yellow L-shape) and the charcoal shape both look like L-shapes. 
The yellow L-shape appears to be oriented with the horizontal bar on top and vertical bar going down on the left.
The charcoal L-shape appears to be oriented with the horizontal bar on top and vertical bar going down on the left.
They have the same orientation! Maybe the goal is to get the target to the same position as the charcoal shape?
Let me try moving the avatar to the left so that the target (which is opposite to the move direction) ends up near the charcoal shape.
If I move RIGHT, the target will be to the LEFT of the avatar. If the avatar is at the right side, the target would be on the left side, potentially near the charcoal shape.
Let me try moving RIGHT several times to position the avatar on the right, which should put the target on the left near the charcoal shape.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Track target position after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] > 10 and n['color'] in ['B', 'c', 'Y']:
        b = n['boundary']
        r, c = min([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b])
        print(f"{n['color']}: top=({r},{c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Target is opposite to last move direction
# After LEFT: target is to the RIGHT of avatar
# After DOWN: target was ABOVE avatar
# Hypothesis: target always appears opposite to last move
# If I move RIGHT, target should be to the LEFT of avatar
# Let me try moving RIGHT to put target on left side near charcoal
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols right", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols left", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows down", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows up", "SPACE": "No effect yet", "MOUSE": "No effect (VERIFIED)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar", "charcoal_shape": "Moves with avatar", "yellow_shape": "Target that orbits avatar"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_row": "Black strip", "right_col": "Yellow strip"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Target appears opposite to last move direction", "goal_model": "Maybe get target to overlap charcoal shape?", "recent_findings": ["Target opposite to last move: DOWN->above, LEFT->right"], "open_questions": ["What completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Move RIGHT to put target on left near charcoal, then try SPACE", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["ACTION7", "MOUSE on target", "SPACE when target not adjacent"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
